name: Can I get attorney-client privilege for my chats with Google Gemini about my legal case?
description: No, Heppner case confirms no privilege for consumer AI chats.

mainEntity.name: Can I get attorney-client privilege for my chats with Google Gemini about my legal case?

acceptedAnswer.text: No, Heppner case confirms no privilege for consumer AI chats.
acceptedAnswer.description: No, courts like SDNY in Heppner ruled AI chats lack privilege as AI isn't a lawyer and privacy policies waive confidentiality.[1][4] Outputs using privileged inputs are discoverable. Key factors: AI's third-party status; data training disclosure; not for legal advice. Avoid inputting case details into public AI tools if litigating; use lawyer-supervised AI only. This applies nationwide post-Heppner.
